title: Remove unused script file
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```
Signed-off-by: yaozhenxiu <946666800@qq.com>
<!--
1, In the latest `github.com/containernetworking/plugins` code, only `build_linux.sh` exists
```shell
xiu@xiu-VirtualBox:~/podman$ cd /go/src/github.com/containernetworking/plugins
xiu@xiu-VirtualBox:/go/src/github.com/containernetworking/plugins$ ls
bin             build_windows.sh  DCO     go.sum       LICENSE    pkg      README.md     scripts        test_windows.sh
build_linux.sh  CONTRIBUTING.md   go.mod  integration  OWNERS.md  plugins  RELEASING.md  test_linux.sh  vendor
```

2, We must use `make` to compile the `runc` file before execute `make install`
-->

comments.body: @containers/podman-maintainers Is anything using this script? Can this be removed instead? I see little value in providing this script since nobody is maintaining it.
@Luap99 Today is the first time to read the code of podman,I saw this script and used it to initialize an environment for debug code

Sure your contribution is welcome but I think there is not much value in providing this script. The dependencies should all be available via package manager. If building from source is required it is best to check out the projects directly, at least in my opinion.
But lets wait what other maintainers think.

Btw you have to sign your commits, see https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#sign-your-prs
History suggests that this was created for something called "papr", which AFAICT we ended up not using. Let's just nuke it.
Papr was our CI solution prior to Cirrus, which makes it... Three years old? Concur that this is unused and should just be removed.

LGTM but you still need to sign your commit: 
```console
$ git commit --amend -s
...
$ git push --force
```
Thank you!
@edsantiago Thank you, is it okay now?

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe*, *yzxiu* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
